LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE DEC-30-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ON DECEMBER 10, 2024, THE COURT GRANTED THIS MOTION AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT HAS SUBSEQUENTLY LEARNED THAT AN AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND A CONDITIONAL NON-OPPOSITION WAS FILED. THE COURT VACATES THE DECEMBER 10, 2024, RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS. NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS'S REQUEST FOR RECOVERY OF FEES AND COSTS AGAINST CCSF'S COUNSEL IS DENIED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/CK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2025 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Continued To Dec-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Quash Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Northwestern Medicaine And Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, Etc.
Off Calendar Jan-08-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NANCY WEBBER's Motion To Quash Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To Northwestern Medicaine And Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, Etc. is granted in part.

1. CCSF's subpoenas, which properly seek medical information in this personal injury action, are overbroad.

2. They seek "all" medical and insurance records with no time limitation. (See Decl. Graziano, 6 Exh. B.)

3. Recognizing the overbreadth of the subpoenas and problems with withdrawing and reissuing them, the parties agreed that a First Look Agreement could resolve the discovery dispute. (See Decl. Graziano, 10 Exh. F.)

4. The Parties, however, could not agree on how long the review process would take. As a new trial date has been set the issue is now moot.

5. There is no longer an impediment to the parties implementing a First Look Agreement.

6. The Parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the precise scope of the First Look Agreement as contemplated in plaintiff's October 23, 2024, letter. (See Decl. Graziano, 7 Exh. C.)

7. The parties shall fashion the agreement by January 22, 2025. If issues remain outstanding, then either party may file a clarifying motion by January 29, 2025.

8. Request for sanctions/denied. The Court finds the parties acted with substantial justification.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302/Discovery.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Text Ruling

Amended MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC's Amended MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

On December 10, 2024, the court granted this motion as unopposed. The court has subsequently learned that an amended notice of motion and a conditional non-opposition was filed. The court vacates the December 10, 2024, ruling and rules as follows.

New Cingular Wireless's motion to set aside default judgment is GRANTED as unopposed.

New Cingular Wireless's request for recovery of fees and costs against CCSF's counsel is DENIED.

Plaintiff's original complaint named the movant, New Cingular Wireless (NCW), Pacific Bell, and CCSF as defendants in this personal injury action. The City and County of San Francisco filed a cross-complaint on March 1, 2024, naming NCW and Pacific Bell separately. On October 10, 2024, the clerk entered default against NCW for failing to answer CCSF's cross-complaint.

Pacific Bell's filings designated itself as "erroneously sued as New Cingular Wireless." CCSF counsel and counsel for both NCW and Pacific Bell discussed an agreement to accept service of the cross-complaint on behalf of NCW, as well as an agreement relating to NCW's liability. Agreement was not reached before CCSF secured an entry of default.

NCW then moved to set aside that default. The motion was heard initially on December 10, 2024, and granted as unopposed. Before the hearing, the NCW filed an amended notice of motion, continuing the matter to December 30, 2024. The court was unaware of the amended notice as no party informed the court.

A conditional non-opposition now opposes the request for sanctions and costs.

CCP section 128.5 allows an award of cost and attorney's fees if they are incurred "as a result of actions or tactics, made in bad faith, that are frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay." (CCP section 128.5(a).) The statute defines actions or tactics as "included, but not limited to," making or opposing motions or service of a pleading. (Id. at (b)(1).) Thus, CCSF's argument that the code is "confined solely" to those two actions is plainly incorrect. (Conditional Non-Opposition, 7.)

Section 128.5 requires a finding of bad faith. The issue is arguable but on balance the showing here is not made out. Compare e.g., Shapell Socal Rental Properties, LLC v. Chico's FAS, Inc. (2022) 85 Cal.App.5th 198, 213 (lawyer breached ethical and statutory duties in securing default judgment without contacting opposing counsel). Taking unnecessary defaults and subsequent aggressive actions against a defaulting presents great dangers for a plaintiff or cross complainant. E.g., LCPFV, LLC v. Somatdary Incorporated (2024) 106 Cal.App.5th 743.
I do not find bad faith here. CCSF was not obliged to agree to a proffered stipulation regarding NCW's liability.

The breakdown of communication between counsel does not show bad faith, and the request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/CK)
